entry.title: 1° Meetup SAP Community Triângulo Mineiro
entry.content:
🚀 Está nascendo algo novo no Triângulo Mineiro.

No dia 07/07/2025, vamos realizar o 1º meetup presencial da Comunidade SAP Triângulo Mineiro, em Uberlândia, a partir das 19h.

A proposta desse encontro é simples, mas com grande potencial: reunir pessoas da região que trabalham com SAP ou têm interesse na tecnologia, para trocar experiências, se conhecer e começar a construir juntos o que queremos ver em uma comunidade local.

Não teremos uma palestra ou tema técnico específico nesta primeira edição. Mas isso não significa falta de conteúdo.
Pelo contrário — acreditamos que o conteúdo surge do encontro entre as pessoas certas, e que boas conversas podem gerar grandes iniciativas.

O evento será presencial, descontraído, mas com o devido respeito à proposta profissional. Um espaço para quem quer ouvir, contribuir, compartilhar e se conectar com o ecossistema SAP.

🎯 Se você está em Uberlândia ou arredores e tem alguma ligação com SAP — seja como consultor, usuário, estudante, gestor ou entusiasta — esse convite é pra você.

🔗 https://www.sympla.com.br/evento/1-meetup-sap-community-triangulo-mineiro/2992534?share_id=2-copiarlink

Se quiser participar ou saber mais, me chama por aqui ou comenta neste post.

  • Completed with Error
  • Completed with Warning

Thanks

Ashish

entry.title: Material Document Overview Fiori App features
entry.content:
Inventory Management:

  • Identifying and Addressing Stock Discrepancies: By quickly locating material documents related to unexpected stock level changes, warehouse managers can investigate potential mis-postings. This leads to more accurate inventory records and correct account updates. Sometimes, enhancements made to any logistics module can lead to incorrect postings of goods, even when there are no actual movements of those goods. We can identify such movements.
  • Optimizing Storage and Warehouse Layout: Analyzing the frequency and types of material movements can reveal bottlenecks or inefficiencies in warehouse layout and storage strategies. For example, frequently moved materials can be placed in more accessible locations.
  • Evaluating Inventory Turnover: By reviewing the history of goods issues for specific materials, businesses can assess their inventory turnover rate and identify slow-moving or obsolete stock, prompting decisions on markdowns or disposal.
  • Planning for Stock Transfers: Understanding past stock transfer patterns can help optimize future inter-plant or inter-storage location transfers, ensuring timely availability of materials where needed and minimizing transportation costs.
  • Assessing the Impact of Goods Receipts: Reviewing recent goods receipts helps confirm the timely and accurate arrival of purchased materials, allowing for proactive communication with vendors if issues arise.

Production Planning and Execution:

  • Troubleshooting Production Issues: If production orders face material shortages, the app can quickly show when and why the required materials were issued or if there were any unexpected goods issues that depleted stock.
  • Analyzing Material Consumption: Production planners can review plant-wise material consumption. Which plant consumes more material? When did the plant consume the material? We can observe consumption patterns. The production order number is a field on the selection screen where the user can track material consumption against a particular production order or a certain number range of orders.
  • Tracking Rework and Scrap: Material documents associated with rework or scrap can be easily identified, providing data for analyzing the causes and costs of quality issues. We have separate movement type for scrap which can help in tracking.

Procurement:

  • Verifying Goods Receipt Against Purchase Orders: Procurement teams can quickly check if goods receipts have been posted for their purchase orders, ensuring timely payment processing and vendor performance monitoring. GR can be tracked by movement type 101. Purchase order is a field available on the selection screen where the user can track the GR against a particular PO.
     
  • Investigating Discrepancies Between Ordered and Received Quantities: If there are discrepancies, the material documents provide the details needed to investigate with the vendor and resolve any issues.
     
  • Evaluating Vendor Delivery Performance: Analyzing the timeliness of goods receipts related to specific vendors can contribute to vendor performance evaluations.
  • Vendor return tracking: Vendor returns can be tracked separately with movement types 122 and 161.

Sales and Distribution:

  • Tracking Material Issues for Sales Orders: Customer service and logistics teams can quickly verify when goods were issued for specific sales orders, helping them respond to customer inquiries about shipment status. Movement type 601 can be used here.
  • Investigating Delivery Issues: If a customer reports a missing item, the material document overview can help trace the goods issue and identify potential errors in picking or shipping.
  • Analyzing Returns: Material documents related to customer returns offer analyses of the reasons for returns and the quantities involved, informing decisions on product quality or process improvements.
     

Finance and Accounting:

  • Reconciling Inventory Records: Accountants can use the app to verify the accuracy of inventory postings and reconcile physical inventory counts with system records.
  • Auditing Material Movements: The detailed audit trail provided by material documents is crucial for internal and external audits, ensuring compliance and financial accuracy.
  • Analyzing Material Costs: While this app doesn't directly display costs, the material document information is a key input for cost accounting processes, helping to track material consumption and value flow.

In essence, the "Material Document Overview" Fiori application empowers various business users to:

  • Gain immediate visibility of past and present inventory movements.
  • Check the consumption of material at the plant and storage level.
  • Identify and investigate anomalies or discrepancies in material flow.
  • Access the underlying details of material-related transactions.
  • Make data-driven decisions to optimize their specific processes and contribute to overall supply chain efficiency.

By providing easy access to this critical information, the app fosters a more proactive and informed approach to managing materials across the organization.

entry.title: Payment term for a planned delivery cost vendor
entry.content: Sometimes vendors for planned delivery costs are different than the vendor for the original (main) purchase order; is there a way to set/define the payment terms for this other vendors?.

entry.title: Unable to Add Subaccount to Cloud Connector – Certificate Signing Error
entry.content:
Dear SAPians,

I'm encountering an issue while trying to add a subaccount to the SAP Cloud Connector. The following error appears in the logs:

Failed to sign the Cloud Connector certificate for subaccount 08d249**********85@cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com. Verify configuration and proxy settings. See 'Log and Trace Files' and in particular scc_core.trc for details. Associated entries were logged on or around 2025-06-11 02:04:48,693 +0530. Consult SAP note 2460641 for possible remedies.

Proxy settings appear to be Direct Access (no proxy).Its a trial account and Cloud Foundry environment (not Neo)

--Has anyone faced a similar issue with Cloud Foundry subaccount setup in Cloud Connector?Any guidance or troubleshooting tips are highly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Enhancement in VF01 SAP public cloud
entry.content:

requirement is to store the invoice values into a custom table and and send to the invoice details to external API.

how to achieve this .

Do we have to create a BADI or any exist available in Public cloud as in ECC or S4HANA system

or do we have any other alternatives?

please suggest.

Guru

entry.title: Re: LMS Organization Connector File - All Levels
entry.content:
Hi @MelanieBelzile 

Yes, it can be done with importing  the Employee Central organization structure could/can combine more than one level such as with the following example:

(Level 0 (Legal Entity),

Level 1(Business Unit),

Level 2 (Division), and

Level 3 (Department)),

as such this can be then replicated the same in LMS by creating a job in Integration Center. This way LMS will build the organization structure as well as updating the descriptions and respecting the Employee Central hierarchy.

The structure of this Integration Center job differs because it is needed to build the entire organization structure where there is a Parent Org assigned to each child, to reflect the same as in Employee Central. To comply with this format, the Integration Center job should be created as a Stacked/EDI file with four rows.

The number of columns is determined by the number of fields that will be used in the LMS. An Org file must contain fields for the Organization ID (ORG_ID), Organization Description (ORG_DESC), and the Parent Organization
(ORG_ID_PARENT).
An example of the layout is below. The Header Line contains the LMS DB field names. These field names will correspond to the field names in the CONNECTOR properties file.  The Starting Entity for this job must be the lowest level. In the example below, we are using the Department.

There is a really good IDP on this called "SuccessFactors Suite Impact of Implementing Employee Central for LMS Customers_V4" that covers this really well.

Diego

entry.title: Re: Enhancement in VF01 SAP public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can't enhance VF01 directly.
Instead,

  • Use BAdI SD_BILLING_STANDARD (via Custom Logic app) to capture invoice data.
  • Store values in a custom business object or table.
  • Use outbound communication (e.g. event-based or HTTP call) to send data to the external API.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Purchasing tax for Canada import
entry.content:
Hello Lucie,

Thank you for the feedback 

There is no SSCUI that directly defines "imports", instead, use

  • SSCUI 103322 to maintain the tax rate,

Make your access sequence and condition table include the Import/Export Indicator (TSTL) to make import-specific rates work.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Temporary Deactivation and Future Synchronization of Asset Creation for Equipment Records
entry.content:
Currently, our system is configured to synchronize equipment and fixed asset master records. When an equipment record is created, a corresponding asset master record is also created. Additionally, changes to certain fields in the equipment record will update the respective asset master data. However, the system is not set up to create equipment records upon the creation or editing of asset records.

Recently, we received a request from the business to temporarily deactivate the automatic creation of asset records when equipment records are created. To accommodate this, we will change the configuration to "No synchronization."

The question now is: how can we recreate asset records when the business requests synchronization again? Aside from manually creating assets and linking them via LSMW, is there another method to automatically create asset records for the equipment?

I attempted to change the configuration to "Create asset = Direct synchronization after saving," but it did not result in the creation of an asset record.

Appreciate your thoughts and ideas.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Eliminate EC data freeze period during compensation p...
entry.content: @vinkamath Most customers will use an EC effective date in the past from the moment they start working on launching the worksheets, which is also our leading practice. If your salary review starts in March and you start making changes in mid-february, have data (and eligibility as a consequence most of the time) be as of Feb 1 or Jan 1. Or at the latest on the day you launch the cycle. Which means that yes we recommend to freeze data as of a date in the past. If people's manager changes however we would still want people moved from one worksheet to another during the cycle which is still possible since the template effective date doesn't apply to the MANAGER or SECOND_MANAGER columns. Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Industry fields in the Customer/Supplier master data...
entry.content:
Hi Lucie,

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, industry fields created via SSCUIs apply to Business Partner master data, but are not shown by default in the "Supplier Line Items" app.


To display,

If added via Custom Fields and Logic, ensure the field is

  • Enabled for analytics
  • Assigned to the current business context (e.g., Supplier Line Items)

Otherwise, create a Custom CDS View that joins supplier line items with BP data (including your industry field), and build a Custom Analytical Query on top.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Project gets saved under unsorted in Manager
entry.content:
Hi Sanchia,

When an author creates a new content object in Producer, e.g. a Simulation, they typically obtain editing control of the new object, but also the parent folder (Group) object of the new object.  The author must give back editing control of the parent folder object, not just the new content object that they create.  Manager must be able to get editing control of the parent folder object to write the relationship between the folder and the new content object or the new content object will appear in the Unsorted folder in Manager.  So, as a rule, all authors should give up editing control - Finish Editing - of all content and folder objects when they are not editing them.  This will ensure that the content is displayed in the correct part of the content structure in Manager.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud CCv2 - SolrCloud Instance recommen...
entry.content:
Hello,

There are too many factors could affect the memory usage (e.g. replica number, doc size...). So the "actual" memory usage need depends on your load testing result.

There are some known configurations which could reduce the memory usage, please consider review them:
3341310 - Solr server consuming too much CPU/Memory resources 
Best regards,

Kevin

I'm using a Trial account and I haven't created any instances yet.

Does anyone know how to solve this problem?

entry.title: Re: If I run the automation in test mode of BPA, will it...
entry.content:
Hi @manish_09,

Thanks for the information!

I understood that running and testing in test mode will not charge any cost

and only after deploying and running the project in unattended or attended mode will charge cost.

 

If so, could you please share me the information about how connection count will be calculated too?

for example, we will use attended mode and we have 5 users.

Scenario 1,

In this scenario, we will need to install desktop agent in every user's local machine (eg. PC). Is it correct?

And every user will open the desktop agent and run the same project from their local machine.

For this scenario, will attended mode count be calculated as 5 (or) 1 (or) every time they run the process?

 

Scenario 2,

we will have virtual machine and desktop agent will be installed in that virtual machine.

Every user will enter to virtual machine and run the process from desktop agent of virtual machine.

For this scenario, how the count will be calculated?

 

Could you please explain it to me?

 

Thanks and best regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Re: Realized Conversion Diff Loss account in Outgoing Payments.
entry.content: Hi Joseph,

entry.title: Streamlining Privileged Access in SAP: Practical Tips for Reliable Session Management
entry.content:
For enterprises running SAP at scale, privileged access is both a necessity and a significant risk. Whether responding to production incidents, executing sensitive financial transactions, or configuring core systems, elevated access must be tightly governed. Without robust controls, organizations risk compliance violations, audit gaps, and unauthorized exposure of sensitive data. 

This post highlights how SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG) can help your organization secure, monitor, and audit privileged access, with practices tailored to meet the scrutiny of internal auditors, external regulators, and executive risk committees. 

 

Common Use Cases  

When to Assign Privileged Access 

PAM IDs are not for routine tasks. They should be reserved for high-risk or time-sensitive operations such as: 

  • Urgent Fixes – for example responding to production outages. 
  • System Setup or Configuration Changes – including user management or master data updates. 
  • Sensitive Transactions in Regulated Areas – such as finance or healthcare, where full traceability is required. 

Where to Use PAM IDs 

Not all systems require the same level of access control. Here’s how to prioritize: 

  • Production Systems: Always enforce PAM to protect live data. 
  • Development/Test Systems: Use PAM for high-impact changes, while allowing standard accounts for daily work. 

 

Best Practices 

Synchronizing PAM Logs 

Reliable synchronization between SAP ABAP systems and SAP IAG ensures that every privileged session is logged, reviewed, and auditable. 

Recommendations: 

  • Sync Interval: Schedule synchronization jobs every 6 to 12 hours to achieve near real-time visibility without overburdening the system. 
  • Avoid Over-Syncing: Running sync jobs too frequently (e.g., every 30 minutes) can lead to job overlaps and potential failures. 
  • Optimize Timing: Execute jobs during off-peak hours (e.g., midnight) to reduce system load and avoid conflicts with business operations. 
  • Proactive Monitoring: Regularly monitor sync jobs and review log files to detect errors or delays early and prevent data gaps. 

Tip: In high-volume environments, a single sync job may take over an hour. Plan sync windows accordingly to avoid cascading delays. 

The Privileged Access Log Sync job scans all sessions tied to PAM assignments across connected systems, including both active and expired sessions (within the last six months). This ensures continuity—even if a system was temporarily unavailable or if one system’s sync succeeded while another failed. 

Note: As of the last release, the PAMLOGSYNC - LAST_SYNC_DATE_TIME setting has been removed from the Configuration app, simplifying setup and reducing sync inconsistencies. 

 

Pitfalls in Session Handling: The “Unlock” Problem 

A common issue in customer environments is improper session termination. Many users do not select "Unlock" button in the SIAG_PAM_LAUNCH_PAD after completing their task. This leads to: 

  • Sessions remain open for hours—or even for days. 
  • Overlapping sessions under the same PAM ID. 
  • Inaccurate audit trails and failed sync jobs. 

Quick Fix: Implement SAP Note 3604073 to fix missing logged off timestamps. This ensures sessions are properly closed, even when users forget to terminate them manually. 

 

Additional Recommendations 

For improved performance and session accuracy, we recommend applying the following SAP Notes: 

  • 3606297 – Configure sync intervals for better log preparation. 
  • 3586699 and 3586925 – Enable paging for PAM access logs to boost sync reliability. 

 

Final Takeaway 

When used effectively, Privileged Access Management in SAP IAG is more than a compliance checkbox—it’s a strategic layer of defense. With proper assignment policies, smart sync scheduling, and user discipline, you can ensure privileged sessions are secure, auditable, and well-managed. 

Want to learn more about implementing PAM in your SAP landscape? Contact us or explore the latest updates in SAP Help Portal. 

 

Author(s) 

  • Yuliia Shpak 
  • Swetta Singh

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to get a report showing all login attempts, remot...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please kindly access the Remote Connectivity Logbook in SAP for Me and check the KBA 2529379 about the detailed introduction about this logbook. 

Hope this could meet your requirement.

Best regards,
Joanna Zhao

entry.title: Re: How to Identify Your SAP BTP Global Administrator in SAP for Me
entry.content: Hi Fernanda, this is essential to customers and thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: If I run the automation in test mode of BPA, will it...
entry.content:
Hi @ayechan,

For scenario 1 - The attended mode count will be calculated based on the number of unique desktop agents connected to BTP within a month. If five users each run the process from their own machine, it will count as 5 connections.

For scenario 2 - The attended mode count will be calculated based on the single desktop agent on the single virtual machine. Regardless of how many users access the virtual machine and run the project, it will count as 1 connection.

If a user has the "ProcessAutomationDeveloper / Admin" role and logs in, it will count as an Advanced User Connection.

If a user has the "ProcessAutomationParticipant" role and accesses the form or inbox, it will count as a Basic User Connection.

I don't have any link related to how connections are calculated, but you can refer to this pricing page for BPA - https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog/sap-build-process-automation?region=all&tab=service_plan

For Quotas, Restriction and Limit - https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/quotas-restrictions-and-limits
For the costing Of connections - https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/estimator

Regards,

Manish

entry.title: Re: receive a notification when an onboardee completes pe...
entry.content: @user116 it can be custom as per your requirement.

entry.title: Re: Automated ASM Data Load
entry.content:
you need to log in a ticket to support so they can take a look.

usually it means that ALL data has been loaded and after the load was completed some validation failed.

If validation fails it may mean that there is an entry in loaded data that is not passing the test, sometimes correction of that document in downstream/sourcing may resolve the issue.

Depends at which validation there is a failure some materialised views/cached views may not be refreshed too.

So log a ticket and have support to investigate. Don;t be surprised if this resolves yourself because someone updates a transactional document that was causing this error in the meantime.

entry.title: Re: how to extract a portion of the string from the given string
entry.content: are you looking anything like extract string from after first occurance of - until second occurance of -. Substring basically looks for startindex or start and end index

entry.title: Re: Project gets saved under unsorted in Manager
entry.content: TLDR: Finish editing the parent folder of the project as well 😋

entry.title: Re: Best Approach to Trigger SAP CPI Interface Periodical...
entry.content: I would prefer to trigger CPI from S4H via SOAP Adapter. You can write a program (scheduling as background job) and trigger the CPI within the program. The Plant and Storage location can be retrieved from SM30 or standard variant table.

entry.title: SAP Customer Checkout Plugin – Show Popup for Item Discount on Add Sales Item Even
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am a beginner in SAP Customer Checkout plugin development.

I want to implement a feature where, when a user adds a sales item, a popup appears showing the item's details and allowing the user to input a discount percentage. Once the discount is entered, it should be applied to the item in the backend, and the item's price should be updated accordingly.

What I’ve Done:

  • I used a listener on the addSalesItem method (via @PluginAt).

  • I trigger a custom frontend popup (SHOW_DISCOUNT_POPUP) with the item and receipt details.

  • On user input, I call a backend event APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM to apply the discount.

  • I use Java in App.java for backend logic and JavaScript in myplugin.js for the UI popup.

    App.java (Backend Logic):

    Relevant parts:

    • Captures the last added item and broadcasts a SHOW_DISCOUNT_POPUP event.

    • Handles the APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM event from frontend and applies the discount.

      Code snippet (trimmed for brevity):

       

      java
      CopyEdit
      @PluginAt(pluginClass = ReceiptPosService.class, method = "addSalesItem", where = POSITION.AFTER) public void afterMethodCall(...) { // Gets last added item // Builds and broadcasts a JSON event to frontend payload.put("eventName", "SHOW_DISCOUNT_POPUP"); ... } private void applyDiscountToItem(JSONObject payload) { // Applies discount based on articleId and receiptId // Recalculates the receipt }

       

       

      myplugin.js (Frontend JavaScript):

      • Listens for SHOW_DISCOUNT_POPUP

      • Displays a generic input popup to enter discount

      • Sends APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM to backend with articleId, receiptId, and discount

        Code snippet:

         

        js
        CopyEdit
        this.eventBus.push('SHOW_GENERIC_INPUT', { title: 'Add Discount for Item', configuration: [ new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Article ID: ' + articleId, '1', 'Label'), new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Description: ' + materialDescription, '2', 'Label'), ... new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Discount (%)', '5', 'Input', this.discountModel) ], callback: (positive) => { if (positive) { const discountValue = this.discountModel.getValue(); this.pluginService.backendPluginEvent('APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM', { articleId: articleId, receiptId: receiptId, discount: discountValue }); } } });
         

        Issue:

        The popup displays correctly, and discount value is sent to the backend. However, I’m unsure if the discount is being properly applied to the sales item, and the recalculated price is not updating on the frontend. I also intend to trigger a new popup showing the updated price (like SHOW_NEW_PRICE_POPUP), but it’s not working yet.

        Request:

        Can anyone help me:

        1. Confirm if the discount is being applied correctly?

        2. Guide how to show the recalculated new item price in a popup?

        3. Suggest improvements to the current logic?

          Any help or best practices would be much appreciated!

          Thanks in advance.

        4.  

        5.  

      •  

      •  

    •  

  •  

  •  

  •

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting: Why are the elimination journals rev...
entry.content: Hi Zhu, I am sorry I couldn’t understand your point. Could you please elaborate more?

entry.title: SAP Customer Checkout Plugin – Show Popup for Item Discount on Add Sales Item Event
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am a beginner in SAP Customer Checkout plugin development.

I want to implement a feature where, when a user adds a sales item, a popup appears showing the item's details and allowing the user to input a discount percentage. Once the discount is entered, it should be applied to the item in the backend, and the item's price should be updated accordingly.

What I’ve Done:

  • I used a listener on the addSalesItem method (via @PluginAt).

  • I trigger a custom frontend popup (SHOW_DISCOUNT_POPUP) with the item and receipt details.

  • On user input, I call a backend event APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM to apply the discount.

  • I use Java in App.java for backend logic and JavaScript in myplugin.js for the UI popup.

this is my code set

package com.helloworld.Helloworld;
 
import java.io.InputStream;
import java.math.BigDecimal;
import java.security.PublicKey;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.Map;
 
import com.sap.scco.ap.plugin.BasePlugin;
import com.sap.scco.ap.plugin.annotation.PluginAt;
import com.sap.scco.ap.plugin.annotation.PluginAt.POSITION;
import com.sap.scco.ap.plugin.annotation.ListenToExit;
import com.sap.scco.ap.plugin.annotation.ui.JSInject;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.CDBSession;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.CDBSessionFactory;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.IReceiptManager;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.MaterialDAO;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.ReceiptManager;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.ReceiptPosDAO;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.SalesItemPosDAO;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.impl.MaterialDAOImpl;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.impl.ReceiptPosDAOImpl;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.impl.SalesItemPosDAOImpl;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.BaseEntity.EntityActions;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.MaterialEntity;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.PriceDiscountEntity;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.ReceiptEntity;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.SalesItemEntity;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.service.ReceiptPosService;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.util.ui.BroadcasterHolder;
import com.sap.scco.ap.pos.util.ui.UIEventChannelListener;
import com.sap.scco.env.UIEventDispatcher;
import com.sap.scco.util.logging.Logger;
import com.sap.scco.util.CConst;
 
import net.sf.json.JSONObject;
 
public class App extends BasePlugin {
 
    private static final Logger logger = Logger.getLogger(App.class);
    private String itemId;
    private String receiptId;
    private String articleId;
 
    @Override
    public String getId() { return "saleitem"; }
 
    @Override
    public String getName() { return "additem"; }
 
    @Override
    public String getVersion() { return "1.0.0"; }
 
    @Override
    public boolean persistPropertiesToDB() { return true; }
 
    @Override
    public void startup() {
        logger.info("My plugin started!");
        registerEventHandlers();
        super.startup();
    }
 
 
   public  void registerEventHandlers() {
        BroadcasterHolder.INSTANCE.addEventChannelListener(new UIEventChannelListener() {
            
            public void handleEvent(String eventId, JSONObject payload) {
                if ("APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM".equals(eventId)) {
                    // Log the received data
               
               
                articleId = payload.getString("articleId");
                     receiptId = payload.getString("receiptId");
                   //  discount = payload.getString("discount");
 
                     // Log the values to check assignment
                     logger.info("Assigned articleId: " + articleId);
                     logger.info("Assigned receiptId: " + receiptId);
                    // logger.info("Assigned discount: " + discount);
               
                   
                    logger.info("DEBUG: Received APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM with payload: " + payload);
                  applyDiscountToItem(payload);
                }
            }
        });
    }
    
    
    
    
    @PluginAt(pluginClass = ReceiptPosService.class, method = "addSalesItem", where = POSITION.AFTER)
    public void afterMethodCall(Object proxy, Object[] args, Object returnValue, StackTraceElement caller) {
        logger.info("--------------------***------------------");
      
        MaterialEntity mtt =new MaterialEntity();
        
 
        
        if (args[0] != null && args[1] != null) {
            if (args[0] instanceof ReceiptEntity && args[1] instanceof MaterialEntity) {
            //ReceiptEntity objP = new ReceiptEntity();
   
           
            //objP.addSalesItem(null);
                ReceiptEntity currentReceipt = (ReceiptEntity) args[0];
                
                // Check if sales items list is NOT empty
                if (!currentReceipt.getSalesItems().isEmpty()) {
                    // Get last sales item
                    SalesItemEntity lastSaleItem = currentReceipt.getSalesItems() .get(currentReceipt.getSalesItems().size() - 1);
                       
                    
                    if (lastSaleItem != null) {
                        try {
                            MaterialEntity material = lastSaleItem.getMaterial();
                            
                            
                            this.itemId = lastSaleItem.getExternalId(); // Set global variable
                            this.receiptId = currentReceipt.getId(); // Set global variable
                            
                            // Build the event payload for the frontend popup
                            JSONObject payload = new JSONObject();
                            
                            payload.put("type", "event");
                            payload.put("eventName", "SHOW_DISCOUNT_POPUP");
                            
                            JSONObject eventPayload = new JSONObject();
                            eventPayload.put("itemId", lastSaleItem.getExternalId()); //  Use internal ID for backend
                            eventPayload.put("articleId", material.getExternalID()); // For display
                            eventPayload.put("itemPrice", lastSaleItem.getGrossAmount()); // Gross price
                            eventPayload.put("materialDescription", material.getDescription());
                            eventPayload.put("receiptId", currentReceipt.getId()); // Use internal receipt ID
                            
                            payload.put("eventPayload", eventPayload);
 
                            BroadcasterHolder.INSTANCE.getBroadcaster()
                                .broadcastActionForPath("/ws/tech", "plugin", payload);
                            
                        } catch (Exception e) {
                            logger.info("Error processing last sales item: ", e);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
       
    }
    
 
    
    
    private void applyDiscountToItem(JSONObject payload) {
 
   
        CDBSession session = null;
        try {
           String articleId = payload.getString("articleId");
           String receiptId = payload.getString("receiptId");
            BigDecimal discountPercent = new BigDecimal(payload.getString("discount"));
            
        
 
            logger.info("Looking up receiptId: " + receiptId + ", articleId (external): "+ "    " + articleId + "    " + discountPercent);
 
            session = CDBSessionFactory.instance.createSession();
            
            
 
            ReceiptPosDAO receiptDao = new ReceiptPosDAOImpl(session);
            MaterialDAO sales = new MaterialDAOImpl(session);
            SalesItemPosDAO salesItemPosDAO  = new SalesItemPosDAOImpl(session);
 
            ReceiptEntity receipt = receiptDao.findReceiptById(receiptId);
            
            
            ReceiptEntity idString = receiptDao.findReceiptById(receiptId);
            logger.info("---+" + idString);
            
            
            salesItemPosDAO.delete(receiptId);
            logger.info(receiptId);
            
            MaterialEntity materialEntity = sales.findMaterialByExternalId(articleId, true);
      
            
            if (receipt != null) {
            List<SalesItemEntity> itemsEntities = salesItemPosDAO.getSalesItemsOfReceipt(receipt);
            for (SalesItemEntity item : itemsEntities) {
                logger.info("--(*SalesItemEntity: ID=" + item.getId() +
                            ", Description=" + item.getDescription() +
                            ", Price=" + item.getGrossAmount());
            }
           
           
           
               SalesItemEntity targSalesItemEntity =null;
                   for (SalesItemEntity item : itemsEntities) {
                       MaterialEntity material = item.getMaterial();
                       if (material != null && articleId.equals(material.getExternalID())) {
                       logger.info("**********material");
                        targSalesItemEntity = item;
                           break;
                       }
                   }
           
            if (targSalesItemEntity != null) {
            BigDecimal grossAmount = targSalesItemEntity.getGrossAmount();
            BigDecimal discountAmount = grossAmount.multiply(discountPercent)
                            .divide(BigDecimal.valueOf(100), 2, BigDecimal.ROUND_HALF_UP);
                    logger.info("-----)" + grossAmount + discountAmount + "  " + discountPercent );
            }
           
            logger.info("---*Looking up receiptId: " + receiptId + ", articleId (external): "+ "    " + articleId + "    " + discountPercent);
            logger.info(" ---**" + receipt.getId() + articleId + materialEntity.getExternalID() + ")");
 
           
            if (receipt != null && articleId != null) {
                    // Calculate discount amount
                BigDecimal discountAmount = materialEntity.getPriceDiscount().getDiscount();
 
                logger.info(" ---**" + receipt.getId() + articleId + (materialEntity != null ? materialEntity.getExternalID() : "null") + ")");
               
                targSalesItemEntity.setDiscountAmount(discountAmount);
                  targSalesItemEntity.setDiscountManuallyChanged(true); 
                  targSalesItemEntity.setMarkChanged(true);
                  
                  
                
                   SalesItemPosDAOImpl.updateDB();
                   ReceiptManager receiptManager = new ReceiptManager(session);
                    receiptManager.calculate(receipt, EntityActions.UPDATE);
                    receiptManager.update(receipt);
                    
                }
           
           
           
            } else {
                logger.info("Receipt not found for ID: " + receiptId);
            }
            
            
            
            
 
            //itemId.setDiscountAmount(discountAmount);
            ;
            
            
            
 
        } catch (Exception e) {
            logger.info("Error in applyDiscountToItem: ", e);
        } finally {
            if (session != null) session.closeDBSession();
        }
    }
    
    
    @JSInject(targetScreen = "NGUI")
    public InputStream injectJs() {
        return this.getClass().getResourceAsStream("/resources/myplugin.js");
    }
       
    
    
}
 
this is js code 
 

Plugin.helloworld = class Helloworld {

constructor(pluginService, eventBus) {

this.pluginService = pluginService;

this.eventBus = eventBus;

this.discountModel = new cco.InputModel();

 

this.eventBus.subscribe(this);

this.subscribeToNewPricePopup();

}

 

handleEvent(event) {

if (event.getType() === 'SHOW_DISCOUNT_POPUP') {

const payload = event.getPayload();

this.showDiscountPopup(

payload.itemId,

payload.articleId,

payload.receiptId,

payload.itemPrice,

payload.materialDescription

);

}

}

 

showDiscountPopup(itemId, articleId, receiptId, itemPrice, materialDescription) {

this.discountModel.setValue('');

 

this.eventBus.push('SHOW_GENERIC_INPUT', {

title: 'Add Discount for Item',

configuration: [

new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Article ID: ' + articleId, '1', 'Label'),

new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Description: ' + materialDescription, '2', 'Label'),

new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Item Price: ' + itemPrice, '3', 'Label'),

 

new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Item ID: ' + itemId, '4', 'Label'),

new cco.GenericInputConfiguration('Discount (%)', '5', 'Input', this.discountModel)

],

showKeyboardSwitchButton: true,

keyboardType: 'keyboard',

widthFactor: 1.1,

heightFactor: 1.1,

callback: (positive) => {

if (positive) {

const discountValue = this.discountModel.getValue();

console.log('Sending discount to backend:', discountValue);

 

 

 

this.pluginService.backendPluginEvent('APPLY_DISCOUNT_TO_ITEM', {

articleId: articleId,

 

receiptId: receiptId,

discount: discountValue

});

}

}

});

}

 

 

subscribeToNewPricePopup() {

this.eventBus.subscribe({

handleEvent: (event) => {

if (event.getType() === 'SHOW_NEW_PRICE_POPUP') {

const payload = event.getPayload();

console.log('DEBUG: SHOW_NEW_PRICE_POPUP payload:', payload);

this.eventBus.push('SHOW_MESSAGE_BOX', {

type: 'info',

message: 'Discount applied! New price: ' + payload.newPrice

});

}

}

});

}

};

entry.title: Subcontracting value should be added in material price while doing GRN
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Below is the requirement of customer.

I am sending material XX (price 1000 INR) of 1 Kg for subcontracting to supplier, having PO price 200 INR. And receiving same material XX of 1 Kg in batch no. 123. At the time of GRN inventory price of that particular material (batch 123) should be 1200 INR (price of material + subcontracting charges). Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA public cloud? If yes, how can we achieve this?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Mass Creation of Purchase Orders （6VW)
entry.content:
Hello @Li_Grace 

To create a standard PO item, leave the Item Category field blank in the Excel template. S/4HANA Public Cloud assumes "Standard" if the field is empty.

Only use values like 1 (subcontracting) or 3 (services) if you work with those specific item types. For standard material POs, no entry is needed.

Scenario

Recommended Input

Standard PO Item

Leave Item Category field blank

Subcontracting

Enter 1

Service PO

Enter 3

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: If I run the automation in test mode of BPA, will it...
entry.content:
Hi @manish_09,

Thank you very much for your explanation!

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Enable selection mode in Fiori Elements
entry.content:
I developed a List Report app using Fiori Elements through RAP.

I wanted to enable the selection mode "Single" so I can single record and use a custom action to open a dialog for further processing.

I already added the table setting "selectionMode" into "Single" in the manifest.json but it doesnt reflect in the app.

When I run app and check the table's selection mode, it shows as undefined.

How can I enable the selectionMode "Single" using Fiori Elements?

entry.title: Multiple checkbox data saving in Module pool
entry.content:
Hello Team,

   I am Working in Module pool where i need to replace radio buttons into checkbox and user can select multiple checkboxes from given options, problem is until now there was only one radio button selected and data was saving in that field according to selection mail was triggered to respective department,  but now there is multiple checkboxes user can select so how can i save data for selected checkboxes in one field and send mail according to selected checkboxes. below is the snap of screen 
Anyone having worked on same scenario than please send me Code.

Nature of work

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting value should be added in material pric...
entry.content:
Hello @kranti_babhulkar 

This scenario is possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, which follows the standard subcontracting process.

Your Customer Requirement Recap

  • Send material XX (1 kg, 1000 INR) to a subcontractor.
  • Subcontracting PO has service charge = 200 INR.
  • Expect to receive 1 kg of material XX back in batch 123.
  • Final inventory value of batch 123 should be 1200 INR (material + subcontracting cost).

How to Achieve This in S/4HANA Public Cloud

  • Create a Subcontracting Purchase Order:
    • Use item category L (for subcontracting).
    • Add the component (material XX) in the BOM or component list of the PO.
    • Set the subcontracting charge as a service item (200 INR).
  • Send Component to Subcontractor
    • Post a Transfer Posting using the app: Transfer Stock - Subcontractor.
    • This moves the 1 kg of material XX to the subcontractor stock.
  • Goods Receipt for Subcontracting PO
    • Use the "Post Goods Receipt for Purchase Order" app.
    • During GR, the system consumes the component from the subcontractor's stock.
    • GR is posted for the finished material (XX) with batch 123.
    • The valuation will be:
      • 1000 INR (material component)
      •  
        • 200 INR (subcontracting charge from PO)
      • = 1200 INR inventory value
  • Batch Handling:
    • Ensure Batch Management is active for material XX.
    • At GR, you can enter batch number 123.

 Please make sure

  • The valuation class and price control (typically standard price S) are maintained correctly.
  • If using Split Valuation or Moving Average, the final value posting will reflect subcontracting costs.
  • Apps like “Monitor Material Coverage”, “Manage Purchase Orders”, and “Stock – Single Material” can help validate the flow.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: 🇸🇬 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core ( Singapore )
entry.content: I want to be considered on the waitlist too! Thank you

entry.title: Save public variants in apps
entry.content:
Hi!

Our customer is running a Public cloud system and wants to start creating public variants in reports to make sure everyone doesnt need to do it by them selfs.

As far as we understand that’s included in the admin role, but can you please advice in which catalog this is included? The admin role is so big and we want to limit to only add this function related to public variants if possible.

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: GRACFFUSER table job
entry.content:
Dear Siddesh47,

I'm not really understand your question. The table is updated by a method which fill the neccessary data and it is updated by an OpenSQL command line in the ABAP coding. Can you elaborate more what you would like to know?

Best regards, Mate

entry.title: Re: Unable to access Fiori apps using the Firefighter ID...
entry.content:
Dear Ambedkar,

you are most probable initiating an ABAP Based Firefighter Session, hence no Security Session is created. Please use Web Based Firefighter Session which then creates a proper Web Based Session. For more information please check KBA 2974359.

Best regards, Mate

entry.title: Re: Cost centre not determined during Freight settlement...
entry.content:
Hello @S_Arora

 

Thanks for sharing the screenshots. Based on what I see and your description, here's a breakdown of the two questions,

  1. Why Cost Center is missing in freight cost accruals:
  • The PO or freight condition line doesn't contain a Cost Center, even though account assignment is set to K (Cost Center).
  • SAP doesn’t auto-derive the cost center unless it’s explicitly entered or enhanced via BAdI.
  • You should check the PO or freight settlement doc (FSD) to see if a cost center was passed in the condition line.
  1. How to post freight costs to a WBS/project:
  • Change the Account Assignment Category from K to P (Project) in your MM–TM integration config.
  • Make sure the freight PO or FSD includes the correct WBS Element.
  • If it’s not derived automatically, you can use a BAdI (like BADI_FDCB_SUBCON_ACC) to inject the WBS into the PO lines.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Facing isuue with manage capabilites
entry.content: Error P013: Activation of Trading Partner Management capability has failed. If the issue persists, learn to solve from integration experts by creating a post in the community for SAP Integration Suite.

entry.title: Re: Change of feeder class name in SAP MDG for BP
entry.content:
Hello Colleague,

if the user interface shows an hourglass, you can quick check in transaction SM50 or Browser Developer Console (F12), if the root cause is in the backend (-> ABAP)  or client (-> Java Script).

Best regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: How to Migrate SOAP adapter with Axis protocol from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite(IS)
entry.content: Hi Mahesh, have you got any updates? We are facing the same problem now.

entry.title: Newbie in new SAP Community Group Berlin
entry.content:
Hello everyone, Robby Finke here, and I'm excited to introduce myself. I'm a professional with a background spanning economics, sociology, philosophy, and demography. My journey began in the 1990s as a consultant, where I focused on data analysis, particularly in social welfare, for various public administrations at the municipal and state levels.
The late 90s marked my pivot into the SAP world, starting with an SAP R/2 - R/3 migration project. While that was my entry point, my true passion emerged in data analysis, leading me to specialize in SAP BW. My very first SAP BW project was a prototype at Siemens in Munich. From there, I spent many years, up until 2018, as a dedicated SAP BW consultant—both employed and freelance—supporting a wide array of clients and projects. Interestingly, in parallel (2008-2015), I also built and ran my own successful accounting and payroll business.
Since 2018, I've been contributing to Zalando SE, and since 2020, to Zalando Payments GmbH, in various capacities within SAP BI & Analytics.
It's fantastic news that the SAP Community Group for Berlin is back! I envision this group as a dynamic space for us to share knowledge, learn from each other, and, crucially, connect. I'm especially eager to bring back the Berlin SAP Stammtisch, and I'm looking forward to planning many more engaging events for our community.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to, or are there plans to, bring the N...
entry.content: Yes, Improvement to Quick Apply UI is in Recruiting Product roadmap.

entry.title: Re: 🇸🇬 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core ( Singapore )
entry.content: Thanks for your interest. We shall reach out to you in case of any dropouts.

entry.title: Re: 🇸🇬 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core ( Singapore )
entry.content: Thanks for your interest. We shall reach out to you in case of any dropouts.

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content:
Hi @AnnePetteroe 

I give answers or solutions on a daily basis to various questions and most of them are not closed properly with an update by the member who created the post.

So what I wanted to know is will SCN moderation team monitor this and ask such of those users to close their discussion if the issue is solved with the help of answers provided by the member.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community Berlin
entry.content:
Welcome Antonia from Hamburg to the SAP Group Berlin. I'm very pleased to welcome you and get to know you.
I've already introduced myself in the Welcome Corner (https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-discussions/newbie-in-new-sap-community-group-berlin/td-p/14124692). 😉
I'm already looking forward to the networking opportunities in this group. The prospect of SAP TechEd in November is also great.

entry.title: Re: Automating Customer Communication and Data Delivery Using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
@Anky02 Thanks for writing and sharing as a blog! 

could you modify in your blog for groovy script from plain text into code syntax.. you will see on edit mode - Widget with this icon </>

entry.title: Re: BOM更改凭证无法显示
entry.content:
Hello,

attached you find a link to a knowledge base article, which describes how you can track changes in public cloud.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2788201

Regards Johannes
Johannes Schulte | Senior SAP Expert
Email: johannes.schulte@vico-finconsulting.vicoland.com
On behalf of: Vicoland GmbH
Website: www.vicoland.com/sap

entry.title: SAP BW InfoObject 0DEB_CRE_LC with +/- sign ?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

The standard InfoObject 0DEB_CRE_LC did not click the "Sign". 

And the Currency InfoObject 0LOC_CURRCY did not click the "Sign" , too.

Why 0DEB_CRE_LC still could show the values with + / - sign ? 

Tks.

entry.title: New to the community
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I'm new to the community and I just wanted to quickly introduce myself, I am an Industrial Engineer student from Brazil in the Federal University of São Carlos - Sorocaba Campus, I have finished 3 out of my 5 years, and I am currently in Canada pursuing a diploma in Supply Chain Transportation and Logistics.

Durring my studies in the University the course I mostly enjoyed was Production Planning and Control, and now I am also really enjoying Supply Chain Transportation and Logistics. Those two courses were what brought me here to learn the SAP software. I hope to be able to learn a lot here and use this knowledge on my career in either of those two fields that I really enjoy and hope to work in in the future.

entry.title: Re: Multiple checkbox data saving in Module pool
entry.content:
Hello,

Design a common formatted e mail from the data you have along with all the possible fields and based on the check boxes selected add the receivers mail id's of different departments while using mail send option.

Thanks,

Prakash J.

entry.title: Is it Possible to Show only India Map in SAC stories instead of other country as well.
entry.content: While creating a Map in SAC stories we want to show only India Map but while visualizing other countries are also visible. Is it possible to show Only Legends of India or Only India Map.

entry.title: Re: I want to run a job in ADT for a Custom Object create...
entry.content:
Hi Sasaki,

Can you try the example provided under the document for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? This should not be causing any syntax errors https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/232c4a8ff2d8422b93bc89151068b893.html?q=Application+Jobs

Thanks,
Prasanth

entry.title: Re: Create billing document" not allowed (Sys. status Cos...
entry.content: If you have authorization in va02, you can change the status to overcome from that issue.  But as a permanent solution, I believe, the error log is related to VPRS condition type which would be missing in the credit memo request.  Please check and correct it

entry.title: Data Upload Starter issue in SAC
entry.content:
Hi All,

In the Data upload starter button we have option in edit scripts 

1. On before Execute   2. On After Execute 

We are trying to run one data action after the upload job ran. So we wrote script On After Execute  and it's working perfectly. Now the issue is once you click the Upload starter button and click on cancel instead of Run (As shown in screenshot), still the Data action is executing.  Anybody faced this issue and Is there any way to stop the Data action running If we cancel the upload job.

@William_Yu  @N1kh1l  Could you assist here ?

Thanks,

Ravi

entry.title: How to Set an Image as Background in a Page of an SAP MDK Application
entry.content:
Hello Everyone

I'm currently developing an application using SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) v24.11.3, and I would like to set an image as the background for one of the pages for web platform. Could you please guide me on how to achieve this?

Specifically, I’m looking for:

  • The correct way to configure a background image in a page layout.
  • Supported image formats and any size or performance considerations.
  • Any sample metadata or layout configuration that demonstrates this setup.

Your guidance or reference to relevant documentation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Requirement to Include "From Date" and "To Date" in T...
entry.content:
Hi Felipe,

Thank you for your response.
However, this is not helpful for us. We have already checked and are aware that the PDF version contains these fields. Our requirement is to have these fields available in the Excel export, as we need to modify some data directly in the sheet, which is not possible with a PDF. We also tried converting the PDF to Excel, but the output was not usable for our purpose.
Kindly consider this requirement for the Excel export, as it is essential for our reporting and data handling.
Looking forward to your support.

Regards,
Akshay

entry.title: Re: Task 0 - Server and service provisioning (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content: Hello Hemanth - did you watch the video that covers this (link at the top of this thread), and did you follow the steps? If so, and you're still getting an error, you'll need to help us to help you, by giving much more detail here. What did you do? What are the details? What about screenshots? What else did you try? Thanks. DJ

entry.title: Re: Adding mail text in email - IS-U invoice through Email
entry.content:
Hi,

create new application form that will be collection application form. It will include your current application form (for creating PDF) and new application form that will be used for body of e-mail (in smartform create text that you want to be in body of e-mail). 

Please check documentation for additional information: SAP Help

entry.title: Re: Deployments without passwords with SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery
entry.content:
Hi Diego,

I mentioned that somewhere above in the requirements:

  • A user within the Cloud Identity Services tenant who has the developer role on the target space (the space the application should be deployed to).

So yes, you need to add the user on space level.

Best regards, Holger

entry.title: how to add custom fields in requirement management for focused build
entry.content:
Hi SAP,
We have configured the Focused build, Can you please let me know "how to add custom fields in requirement management for focused build".
Please help on this.
Thank you



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: how to add custom fields in requirement management fo...
entry.content: May need to involve consulting service.

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Configuration - Use the Add Time Dimension Function in SAP IBP
entry.content: Well explained, thanks!
